title: laggera divaricata
intro: Laggera divaricata, also known as Congo spinach or bush spinach, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant's leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function. Laggera divaricata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and it is also used in traditional African cooking as a leafy green vegetable.
